section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating Grant Agreement Annex templates as the governing structural schema for proposal writing
Inventing call constraints, scope requirements, expected outcomes, or expected impacts not present in Tier 2B source documents
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The artifact asserts call scope requirements and constraints in the B.3.1 proposal content at multiple points: 'coordination with ADRA and the GenAI4EU central Hub per scope requirement SR-09 and call constraints CC-03/CC-04' (WP1 task T1-01 description); 'coordination with Testing and Experiment Facilities per call constraint CC-07' (WP8 task T8-02 description); 'AI-on-demand platform per scope requirement SR-10 and call constraint CC-05' (WP8 task T8-03 and WP9 task T9-01 descriptions); 'scope requirement SR-09 and call constraints CC-03/CC-04' (Management Structure and Governance paragraph). The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] contains no path within docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/. The only call-constraint traceability present is CLM-020 in validation_status.claim_statuses[], which traces SR-10, CC-05, and CC-07 to Tier 4 (docs/tier4_orchestration_state/phase_outputs/phase6_implementation_architecture/implementation_architecture.json) rather than to Tier 2B extracted files. The complete Tier 2B path is absent from the artifact's primary_sources[], making the call requirement claims untraceable to Tier 2B at the artifact footer level as required by CLAUDE.md §13.2.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Inventing project facts — partner names, capabilities, roles, objectives, prior experience, budget figures, team sizes, or equipment — not present in Tier 3
Commencing any Phase 8 activity — including preparatory drafting — before the budget gate (Phase 7) has passed; absent budget artifacts are a blocking gate failure
Storing durable decisions only in agent memory without writing them to Tier 4
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not checkable from artifact content alone. This prohibition governs runtime agent behavior (whether decisions are persisted to docs/tier4_orchestration_state/decision_log/ or held only in agent memory). A Tier 5 section artifact cannot evidence or refute in-memory decision handling. Enforcement is the responsibility of the decision-log-update skill during workflow execution. Recorded as pass; non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Allowing a workflow or skill to become a de facto constitutional authority by expanding its scope to redefine phase meanings, tier meanings, or gate logic
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not checkable from artifact content. This prohibition governs workflow and skill scope boundaries at the orchestration layer. A Tier 5 proposal section cannot contain evidence of a skill or workflow expanding its scope to redefine constitutional authorities. Recorded as pass; non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Silently reordering phases or weakening gate conditions to allow workflow progress when inputs are incomplete
Finalizing proposal text when source state is known to be incomplete, contradictory, or unvalidated, without explicitly flagging the gap in the deliverable
Using agent-local knowledge of Horizon Europe programme rules as a substitute for reading Tier 1 source documents when those documents are present and accessible
section13_checks.violation_evidence: The B.3.1 Management Structure and Governance content states: 'it is the final internal dispute resolution authority consistent with consortium agreement guidance' — a reference to Horizon Europe consortium agreement terms, which are governed by the Model Grant Agreement (a Tier 1 normative framework document). The traceability_footer.primary_sources[] contains no path within docs/tier1_normative_framework/extracted/; the eight listed primary_sources span only Tier 2A (instrument schemas), Tier 3 (consortium data), and Tier 4 (phase outputs). No Tier 1 normative source is cited in the artifact's footer for any programme-rule reference. Applied conservatively per skill specification constraint (uncertainty defaults to conservative application).

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Producing outputs in Tier 5 that are not traceable to specific inputs in Tier 1–4
Modifying Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents to reflect project-specific assumptions
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not checkable from a phase output or deliverable artifact. Modification of Tier 1 or Tier 2 source documents would be detectable only by inspecting those source files for injected project-specific content. This check requires a dedicated Tier 1/2 source-integrity scan, which is outside the scope of a Tier 5 deliverable audit. Recorded as pass; non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

section13_checks.prohibition_description: Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory or optional
section13_checks.violation_evidence: Not directly checkable from artifact content. Treating CLAUDE.md as advisory would manifest in workflow-layer decisions such as phase reordering, gate bypasses, or tier-authority inversions — none of which are visible in a Tier 5 section artifact's content or traceability footer. Recorded as pass; non-applicable to artifact-level audit.

decision_log_entries.resolution_note: Constitutional violation detected; requires human operator resolution. The implementation section (orch.tier5.implementation_section.v1) asserts call scope requirements (SR-09, CC-03, CC-04, CC-05, CC-07, SR-10) in proposal content across WP1, WP8, WP9 task descriptions and the management structure narrative, but the traceability_footer.primary_sources[] contains no reference to docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/. The call constraint traceability in CLM-020 points only to Tier 4 (implementation_architecture.json). Recommended resolution paths: (1) Add the relevant Tier 2B extracted file path (e.g., docs/tier2b_topic_and_call_sources/extracted/call_constraints.json or equivalent) to traceability_footer.primary_sources[], confirming that SR-09, CC-03/CC-04, CC-05, CC-07, and SR-10 are verifiably present in that Tier 2B file; (2) if Tier 2B extracted files were not populated for these constraint identifiers during Phase 1 call analysis, that constitutes a Phase 1 gate condition failure that must be resolved upstream before this artifact can be considered constitutionally compliant. The artifact must not be considered finalised for submission until this traceability gap is resolved and documented.
